---
title: "Zarr Sources"
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
using GeoDataAccess
using GeoDataAccess: MetaData, ARCOERA5, NASAPowerZarr, GFSZarr,
    AbstractZarrSource, store_url
```

Zarr sources provide **lazy, chunk-on-demand access** to cloud-hosted datasets.  Unlike
download-based sources that use `fetch` to retrieve files, Zarr sources give you a store URL
that you open directly with [Zarr.jl](https://github.com/JuliaIO/Zarr.jl).  Only the chunks
you actually index are transferred over the network.

```julia
using Zarr
url = store_url(ARCOERA5())
store = zopen(url, consolidated=true)
store["2m_temperature"]  # lazy — no data transferred yet
store["2m_temperature"][1, :, :]  # fetches one timestep
```

All Zarr sources are subtypes of `AbstractZarrSource <: AbstractDataSource` and appear in
`all_sources()`.  They define `MetaData` (for variable listings and documentation) and
`store_url` (to get the cloud path).

## ARCO-ERA5

Analysis-Ready, Cloud-Optimized ERA5 reanalysis on Google Cloud Storage.  277 variables,
0.25° resolution, hourly, 1940–present.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Store** | `gs://gcp-public-data-arco-era5/ar/full_37-1h-0p25deg-chunk-1.zarr-v3` |
| **Format** | Zarr v2, consolidated |
| **Dims** | `[time, latitude, longitude]` (surface) or `[time, level, latitude, longitude]` (model-level) |
| **Chunks** | `[1, 721, 1440]` — one timestep, full spatial grid |
| **Auth** | None |
| **Requires** | `Zarr.jl` |

```{julia}
MetaData(ARCOERA5())
```

```julia
using Zarr
store = zopen(store_url(ARCOERA5()), consolidated=true)
t2m = store["2m_temperature"]
```

## NASA POWER Zarr

NASA POWER Analysis-Ready Data on AWS S3.  Multiple products (MERRA-2, SYN1Deg) with
configurable chunking orientation.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Store** | `s3://nasa-power/{product}/{orientation}/power_{product}_{frequency}_{orientation}_utc.zarr` |
| **Format** | Zarr v2, consolidated |
| **Dims** | `[time, lat, lon]` |
| **Auth** | None (anonymous S3, `us-west-2`) |
| **Requires** | `Zarr.jl`, `AWS.jl` |

```{julia}
MetaData(NASAPowerZarr())
```

```{julia}
# Default: MERRA-2 daily, temporal chunking
store_url(NASAPowerZarr())
```

```{julia}
# SYN1Deg hourly, spatial chunking
using GeoDataAccess: NASAPowerZarr
store_url(NASAPowerZarr(product=NASAPowerZarr.Product.syn1deg, frequency=NASAPowerZarr.Frequency.hourly, orientation=NASAPowerZarr.Orientation.spatial))
```

### Orientation

Choose based on your access pattern:

- **`Orientation.temporal`** — chunks are `[5844, 15, 15]` (many timesteps, small spatial tiles).  Best
  for time series at a point or small region.
- **`Orientation.spatial`** — chunks are `[1, 361, 576]` (one timestep, full grid).  Best for spatial
  maps at a single date.

## GFS Zarr (dynamical.org)

NOAA GFS forecast data via dynamical.org.  21 surface variables, 0.25° resolution,
forecasts initialized every 6 hours from 2021 to present.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Store** | `https://data.dynamical.org/noaa/gfs/forecast/latest.zarr` |
| **Format** | Zarr v3 with sharding |
| **Dims** | `[init_time, lead_time, latitude, longitude]` |
| **Auth** | None |
| **Requires** | `Zarr.jl` with v3 support |

```{julia}
MetaData(GFSZarr())
```

::: {.callout-warning}
This store uses **Zarr v3** with sharding codecs.  Verify that your version of Zarr.jl
supports v3 before opening.
:::

```julia
using Zarr
store = zopen(store_url(GFSZarr()))
t2m = store["temperature_2m"]  # [init_time, lead_time, lat, lon]
```
